categories.name: Schema Validation
categories.description: Validates that MCP config files are well-formed JSON with correct structure for their target client.

categories.name: Security
categories.description: Detects hardcoded secrets and insecure transport in MCP config files.

categories.name: Command Validation
categories.description: Validates stdio server commands and file path arguments.

categories.name: Deprecation
categories.description: Flags deprecated MCP transport protocols and patterns.

categories.name: Environment Variables
categories.description: Validates environment variable references for correctness and client compatibility.

categories.name: Cross-File Consistency
categories.description: Compares MCP configs across multiple client config files in the same project.

categories.name: Redundancy
categories.description: Flags unnecessary or stale MCP config entries.

rules.description: MCP config file is not valid JSON.
rules.trigger: JSON.parse fails on the file content.
rules.message: MCP config is not valid JSON: {parseError}

rules.description: Root key does not match the expected key for the target client. VS Code uses "servers"; all other clients use "mcpServers".
rules.trigger: Root key is "mcpServers" in .vscode/mcp.json, or "servers" in any other config file.
rules.message: {file} must use "{expected}" as root key, not "{actual}"
rules.fixDescription: Rename the root key to match the expected key for the client.

rules.description: No recognized root key ("mcpServers" or "servers") found in the config.
rules.trigger: The parsed JSON object has neither "mcpServers" nor "servers" as a key.
rules.message: MCP config has no "{expected}" key

rules.description: A stdio server entry has no "command" field.
rules.trigger: Server has type "stdio" (or no type with no url) and no "command" field.
rules.message: Server "{name}": missing "command" field

rules.description: An HTTP or SSE server entry has no "url" field.
rules.trigger: Server has type "http" or "sse" and no "url" field.
rules.message: Server "{name}": missing "url" field

rules.description: Server type field is not a recognized transport.
rules.trigger: "type" field is present but is not "stdio", "http", or "sse".
rules.message: Server "{name}": unknown transport type "{type}"

rules.description: Server has both a command (stdio) and a URL (http), making the transport ambiguous.
rules.trigger: Server entry has both "command" and "url" fields.
rules.message: Server "{name}": has both "command" and "url" — transport is ambiguous

rules.description: The config file has a root key but no server entries.
rules.trigger: Root key exists but the object is empty.
rules.message: MCP config has no server entries

rules.description: A git-tracked config file contains a literal Bearer token in the Authorization header.
rules.trigger: Authorization header value matches "Bearer <literal>" where the token is not an env var reference, and the file is tracked by git.
rules.message: Server "{name}": hardcoded Bearer token in a git-tracked file
rules.fixDescription: Replace the literal token with an env var reference derived from the server name.

rules.description: A git-tracked config file contains a value matching known API key patterns.
rules.trigger: A header or env value matches known API key prefixes (sk-, ghp_, xoxb-, AKIA, etc.) or is a high-entropy string > 20 chars, and the file is tracked by git.
rules.message: Server "{name}": possible API key in a git-tracked file
rules.fixDescription: Replace the literal value with an env var reference.

rules.description: A git-tracked config URL contains query parameters that look like secrets.
rules.trigger: URL contains query params like ?key=, ?token=, ?api_key= with literal values in a git-tracked file.
rules.message: Server "{name}": possible secret in URL query string

rules.description: Server URL uses HTTP without TLS for a non-localhost target.
rules.trigger: URL scheme is http:// and host is not localhost or 127.0.0.1.
rules.message: Server "{name}": URL uses HTTP without TLS

rules.description: On Windows, npx commands in stdio configs require a cmd /c wrapper to spawn correctly.
rules.trigger: Platform is Windows, command is "npx" (not "cmd" with /c npx in args).
rules.message: Server "{name}": npx requires "cmd /c" wrapper on Windows
rules.fixDescription: Rewrite command from "npx" to "cmd" with ["/c", "npx", ...] args.

rules.description: A stdio command references a local file path that does not exist.
rules.trigger: Command starts with ./ or ../ and the resolved path does not exist.
rules.message: Server "{name}": command "{command}" not found

rules.description: A stdio arg looks like a local file path but the file does not exist.
rules.trigger: An arg matches a file path pattern (contains / with extension, or starts with ./) and the file does not exist.
rules.message: Server "{name}": arg "{arg}" references a missing file

rules.description: SSE transport was deprecated in the March 2025 MCP spec update. Use Streamable HTTP instead.
rules.trigger: Server has "type": "sse".
rules.message: Server "{name}": SSE transport is deprecated — use "http" (Streamable HTTP)
rules.fixDescription: Replace "sse" with "http" in the type field.

rules.description: Environment variable reference uses the wrong syntax for the target client.
rules.trigger: ${VAR} used in Cursor config (should be ${env:VAR}), or ${env:VAR} used in Claude Code config (should be ${VAR}).
rules.message: Server "{name}": {client} uses {expected}, not {actual}
rules.fixDescription: Rewrite env var references to the correct syntax for the target client.

rules.description: A referenced environment variable is not set in the current shell environment.
rules.trigger: Env var reference found but the variable is not in process.env.
rules.message: Server "{name}": environment variable "{var}" is not set

rules.description: Server has an empty env object that can be removed.
rules.trigger: "env" key is present but the object is empty ({}).
rules.message: Server "{name}": empty "env" block can be removed

rules.description: Server URL is not a valid URL.
rules.trigger: URL cannot be parsed by standard URL parser and does not contain env var placeholders.
rules.message: Server "{name}": invalid URL

rules.description: A project-level config (committed to version control) references localhost, which won't work for other team members.
rules.trigger: URL host is localhost or 127.0.0.1 and the file is a project-level config.
rules.message: Server "{name}": localhost URL in project config won't work for teammates

rules.description: Server URL has no path component. Most MCP servers expect a path like /mcp.
rules.trigger: URL path is empty or just "/".
rules.message: Server "{name}": URL has no path — most MCP servers expect /mcp

rules.description: The same server name is configured differently across multiple client config files.
rules.trigger: Server name exists in 2+ config files with different url or command/args values.
rules.message: Server "{name}" is configured differently in {file1} and {file2}

rules.description: A server name appears more than once in the same config file. JSON last-write-wins behavior means only the last definition is used.
rules.trigger: Duplicate key in the mcpServers/servers object.
rules.message: Duplicate server name "{name}" in {file} — only the last definition is used

rules.description: A server in .mcp.json is not present in another client's project config that also exists.
rules.trigger: Server exists in .mcp.json but is absent from .cursor/mcp.json or .vscode/mcp.json (when those files exist).
rules.message: Server "{name}" is in .mcp.json but missing from {file}

rules.description: A server entry is explicitly disabled.
rules.trigger: Server has "disabled": true.
rules.message: Server "{name}" is disabled — consider removing if no longer needed

rules.description: The same server is identically configured at both project and global scope, making the global entry redundant.
rules.trigger: Server with same name and identical config exists at both project and global scope.
rules.message: Server "{name}" is identically configured in {projectFile} and {globalFile}
